meta.data_source: ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 + curated analyst content

alzheimers.name: Alzheimer's Disease
alzheimers.desc: The most expensive disease in the US — $360B annual cost. Anti-amyloid therapies have finally broken through after decades of failure. The race is now who can make treatment safer, more accessible, and effective earlier.
alzheimers.winner: Leqembi (Eisai/Biogen) holds first-mover advantage as the only fully approved anti-amyloid therapy, but donanemab and next-gen SC formulations are intensifying competition.

insights.b: Even modest penetration — limited today by IV infusion logistics — represents billions for early movers.
Amyloid-related imaging abnormalities are the class-wide safety concern limiting uptake. The drug that solves this wins the market.
Leqembi's SC autoinjector BLA could transform access — current IV every-2-weeks at infusion centers limits real-world adoption.

drugs.name: Leqembi
drugs.edge: Only fully approved anti-amyloid. 27% slowing CDR-SB. SC BLA filed for home dosing.
drugs.insight: Full FDA approval July 2023. Slowed decline 27% vs placebo. SC autoinjector BLA under review — home dosing is the commercial inflection that drives adoption beyond academic centers.

drugs.name: Donanemab
drugs.edge: Finite treatment course — stops when plaques cleared. Only drug with defined treatment endpoint.
drugs.insight: FDA approved July 2024. Game-changing differentiator: treatment STOPS once amyloid clears. Only finite-course anti-amyloid. Lilly exploring combination with tau inhibitor for deeper efficacy.

drugs.name: Remternetug
drugs.edge: Monthly SC home injection vs bi-weekly IV clinic. Access and convenience advantage if Phase 3 succeeds.
drugs.insight: Next-gen subcutaneous monthly antibody. If Phase 3 hits, becomes go-to for newly diagnosed patients wanting home treatment. Could cannibalize IV market significantly.

drugs.name: Trontinemab
drugs.mechanism: Anti-amyloid mAb + transferrin receptor brain delivery
drugs.edge: Novel TfR1 brain delivery — faster plaque clearance at lower doses, potentially reducing ARIA risk.
drugs.insight: Roche's differentiated approach shuttles antibody across blood-brain barrier via transferrin receptor. Phase 2 shows faster plaque clearance at lower doses — potentially addressing ARIA safety concern limiting current therapies.

nsclc.name: Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer
nsclc.desc: The most common cancer globally, transformed by targeted therapies and immunotherapy. Resistance mechanisms always emerge — creating endless pipeline opportunity.
nsclc.winner: AstraZeneca's osimertinib dominates EGFR-mutant NSCLC. Amgen's tarlatamab is rewriting SCLC. The post-Tagrisso ADC space is the next major commercial battleground.

insights.b: NSCLC is now 8+ molecularly defined subtypes. Each is a separate market with separate drugs and separate commercial dynamics.
Every targeted therapy eventually fails via acquired resistance. The pipeline focuses increasingly on post-resistance settings and combination strategies.
Antibody-drug conjugates are the hottest class in oncology. HER3-DXd and TROP2-targeting ADCs could become multi-billion dollar drugs in NSCLC alone.

drugs.name: Tagrisso
drugs.edge: Dominant across EGFR+ NSCLC: 1L, adjuvant, unresectable Stage III. LAURA trial HR 0.16.
drugs.insight: The gold standard. LAURA trial (NEJM 2024): HR 0.16 in unresectable Stage III — best hazard ratio in this setting. $5.7B revenue 2023. Every EGFR drug benchmarked against Tagrisso.

drugs.name: Imdelltra
drugs.edge: First-in-class ES-SCLC. DLL3 on 85% of SCLC tumors. ORR 40% in pretreated patients.
drugs.insight: Transforms ES-SCLC — historically treatment-resistant. ORR 40%, OS 14.3 months in heavy pretreated. DeLLphi-304 Phase 3 data 2025 could redefine standard of care in 1L.

drugs.name: Lumakras
drugs.edge: First approved KRAS inhibitor — cracked 40 years of 'undruggable.' KRAS G12C in ~13% NSCLC.
drugs.insight: Historic — first KRAS drug after 40 years of failure. Combination with SOS1 inhibitor in trials to overcome adaptive resistance. Next value creation lives in combinations.

drugs.name: Adagrasib
drugs.edge: Superior CNS penetration vs sotorasib. Active in brain mets — critical in NSCLC where CNS progression is frequent.
drugs.insight: BMS acquired Mirati for $5.8B 2024. Wins vs sotorasib on CNS activity. Combination with cetuximab approved for KRAS G12C CRC — expansion model applies to NSCLC.

drugs.name: HER3-DXd
drugs.edge: HER3 expressed broadly post-osimertinib. Could become standard of care in 2L+ EGFR+ NSCLC.
drugs.insight: Most anticipated NSCLC readout of 2025. Targets post-Tagrisso EGFR+ patients — massive underserved population. AZ paid $6.9B to co-develop. HERTHENA-Lung02 data = potential multi-billion inflection overnight.

myeloma.name: Multiple Myeloma
myeloma.desc: Plasma cell cancer with extraordinary therapeutic innovation — now a bispecific wars battlefield. BCMA and GPRC5D are the hottest targets, with 5+ companies racing to dominate.
myeloma.winner: J&J's teclistamab was first-to-market BCMA bispecific. Talquetamab (GPRC5D) opens a second front. Pfizer and Regeneron are closing fast.

insights.b: BCMA bispecifics compete on the same antigen. GPRC5D offers a differentiated target — critical when BCMA resistance emerges.
Bispecifics moving into combinations with daratumumab and lenalidomide in earlier lines. First Phase 3 combination data will redefine standard of care.
Cytokine release syndrome and opportunistic infections are class-wide concerns. Outpatient step-up dosing protocols are key commercial differentiators.

drugs.name: Tecvayli
drugs.edge: First approved BCMA bispecific globally. Real-world evidence and front-line combination data building.
drugs.insight: FDA approved Sept 2022 — first BCMA bispecific. 63% ORR in MajesTEC-1. Moving to earlier lines — if positive, expands from 5L+ to massive 2L+ market.

drugs.name: Elranatamab
drugs.edge: Monthly maintenance dosing post-induction — best long-term convenience. Pfizer combining with daratumumab.
drugs.insight: FDA approved Aug 2023. 61% ORR. Q1W then monthly dosing is the key differentiator for long-term treatment. MagnetisMM-9 with daratumumab could leapfrog J&J in earlier lines.

drugs.name: Linvoseltamab
drugs.edge: Numerically highest ORR in BCMA bispecific class at 71%. Every-4-week maintenance dosing.
drugs.insight: 71% ORR in LINKER-MM1 — highest in class. Q4W maintenance = least frequent dosing. BLA accepted 2024. Late to market but ORR data and convenience could carve meaningful share.

drugs.name: Talquetamab
drugs.edge: Novel GPRC5D target — active after BCMA therapy failure. J&J holds both BCMA and GPRC5D approvals.
drugs.insight: FDA approved Aug 2023 — first GPRC5D bispecific. 73% ORR. RedirecTT-1 combining talquetamab + teclistamab — dual bispecific could be transformative with no remaining resistance pathway.

drugs.name: Cevostamab
drugs.edge: Third distinct bispecific target (FcRH5) — works when both BCMA and GPRC5D have been used and failed.
drugs.insight: 53% ORR in CELESTIMO. Third bispecific target positions Roche as go-to when all others have been used. In a market moving toward sequential bispecific therapy, a third target has durable commercial value.

obesity.name: Obesity & Metabolic
obesity.desc: The hottest space in all of pharma. GLP-1 agonists have redefined obesity treatment. Every major pharma company is racing to compete with Novo and Lilly.
obesity.winner: Novo Nordisk (Wegovy) and Eli Lilly (Zepbound) are the dominant duopoly. The oral pill race will define the next decade of the market.

insights.b: GLP-1 obesity drugs are projected to become the largest drug class in pharma history. This is a macro event for the entire healthcare system.
Whoever cracks convenient, effective oral dosing at scale captures enormous incremental market share.
GLP-1s now in trials for T2D, heart failure, sleep apnea, NASH, CKD, addiction, and Alzheimer's. Each is a multi-billion dollar opportunity.

drugs.name: Wegovy
drugs.edge: 15% weight loss. SELECT trial: 20% CV risk reduction — first weight loss drug with proven CV benefit.
drugs.insight: Created the modern obesity market. SELECT (NEJM 2023): 20% MACE reduction transformed prescriber/payer perception from lifestyle drug to essential cardiovascular medicine. Indication expansion machine.

drugs.name: Zepbound
drugs.edge: 22.5% weight loss — numerically best-in-class. OSA approved 2024. Direct head-to-head vs Wegovy ongoing.
drugs.insight: SURMOUNT-1: 22.5% weight loss — best in class. OSA approved 2024. SURMOUNT-5 head-to-head vs Wegovy is the most watched obesity trial. GIP dual agonism may preserve lean muscle mass during weight loss.

drugs.name: CagriSema
drugs.edge: ~25% weight loss in Phase 2. Amylin mechanism additive to GLP-1 — may break the 25% ceiling.
drugs.insight: REDEFINE Phase 3 ongoing. If 25%+ weight loss confirmed, Novo maintains best-in-class vs tirzepatide. Amylin combination is mechanistically novel — different satiety pathway.

drugs.name: Retatrutide
drugs.edge: ~24% weight loss Phase 2. Glucagon receptor component increases energy expenditure beyond reduced intake.
drugs.insight: Lilly's next-gen triple agonist. Glucagon component increases metabolic rate — potential for surgical-level 30%+ weight loss. Phase 2: 24.2% dose-dependent loss.

drugs.name: Orforglipron
drugs.mechanism: Oral non-peptide GLP-1 receptor agonist (once daily pill)
drugs.edge: Once-daily oral pill — no injection. Removes #1 patient adoption barrier: needle aversion.
drugs.insight: Most commercially significant obesity pipeline asset. Effective oral GLP-1 removes needle aversion — the single largest patient barrier. Phase 2: ~15% weight loss. Opens GLP-1 to majority who refuse injections.

als.name: ALS — Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis
als.desc: ALS kills motor neurons — most patients die within 2–5 years. Only 2–3 treatments exist with modest benefit. Recent genetic insights are finally creating druggable targets.
als.winner: No clear winner yet. Tofersen (Biogen) for SOD1-ALS is the only genetically-targeted approval. FUS and C9orf72 gene silencing are the most credible next wave.

insights.b: ~10% of ALS is familial with known mutations (SOD1, C9orf72, FUS, TDP-43). These subtypes are now druggable with ASOs and gene silencing.
ALS qualifies for Orphan Drug, Fast Track, and often Breakthrough designation. FDA accepts functional endpoints with smaller trials.
ALS progression is highly variable — making placebo-controlled trials complex. Platform trials are emerging as a more efficient design.

drugs.name: Qalsody
drugs.edge: First genetically-targeted ALS therapy. Proof-of-concept for gene silencing in ALS.
drugs.insight: FDA accelerated approval April 2023. Reduced SOD1 protein and NfL biomarker. Confirmatory trial ongoing. Proves gene silencing works in ALS — platform validation enabling FUS and C9orf72 programs.

drugs.name: ION363
drugs.edge: FUS-ALS is younger-onset and more aggressive. Compassionate use in Jaci Hermstad drove rapid development.
drugs.insight: Developed at unprecedented speed after compassionate use showed clear slowing. FUS-ALS affects patients ~35-40 years old. If Phase 3 positive, validates gene silencing platform broadly for ALS.

drugs.name: ATLAS Trial
drugs.edge: Treating SOD1 carriers BEFORE symptoms — prevention paradigm. Could potentially prevent ALS entirely.
drugs.insight: Treating SOD1 mutation carriers before symptoms appear. If gene silencing before neuron death works, could prevent ALS. Most exciting ALS concept in decades. Will define future of genetic ALS treatment.

drugs.name: AMX0035
drugs.edge: Rebuilding after Relyvrio withdrawal. Underlying mitochondrial biology remains valid.
drugs.insight: Amylyx rebuilding after voluntary Relyvrio withdrawal (PHOENIX Phase 3 negative, Sept 2023). Biology remains credible. High risk given prior failure but different molecular approach.

drugs.name: CNM-Au8
drugs.mechanism: Catalytic gold nanocrystals — neuronal energy metabolism
drugs.edge: Novel mechanism — catalytic nanocrystals boost neuronal energy. Entirely new class if positive.
drugs.insight: Highly novel — catalytic gold nanocrystals improve mitochondrial efficiency in motor neurons. Phase 2 data 2025. Long shot but high upside asymmetry — the kind of binary bet analysts watch carefully.
